defs: Hard to do or to make; beset with difficulty; attended with labor, trouble, or pains; not easy; arduous.
quotes: There is not the strength or courage left me to venture into the wide, strange, and difficult world, alone.
notes: ☞ Difficult implies the notion that considerable mental effort or skill is required, or that obstacles are to be overcome which call for sagacity and skill in the agent; as, a difficult task; hard work is not always difficult work; a difficult operation in surgery; a difficult passage in an author.

defs: Hard to manage or to please; not easily wrought upon; austere; stubborn; as, a difficult person.
To render difficult; to impede; to perplex.
